title: Week 06 - Quantity Relations and Double Enumeration
description: Practice nested loops, plan counting, condition filtering, and best-plan search.

questions.title: Count Ways to Buy total Candies from Two Candy Types

description.background: A student can buy two kinds of candy: fruit candy and chocolate candy. Only whether the two candy counts add up to total matters.
description.task: Count the number of purchase plans where the two candy counts add up exactly to total.
description.output: Return the number of purchase plans.
description.hint: You can try the quantities of the first candy and the second candy separately.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(total)
    local count = 0

    for a = 0, total do
        for b = 0, total do
            if a + b == total then
                count = count + 1
            end
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Count Outcomes Where Two Dice Sum to the Target

description.background: Two ordinary dice each have values from 1 to 6. A player wants to know how many possible outcomes have a sum equal to target.
description.task: Count the number of cases where the two dice values sum to target.
description.output: Return the number of possible cases.
description.hint: Both the first die and the second die need to be enumerated.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(target)
    local count = 0

    for a = 1, 6 do
        for b = 1, 6 do
            if a + b == target then
                count = count + 1
            end
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Count Length-Width Pairs for a Fixed Rectangle Perimeter

description.background: A rectangle has positive integer length and width. Given perimeter, the teacher wants to know how many length-width pairs can make this perimeter.
description.task: Count the length-width pairs whose perimeter equals perimeter.
description.output: Return the number of length-width pairs.
description.hint: Length and width can both be tried one by one.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(perimeter)
    local count = 0

    for length = 1, perimeter do
        for width = 1, perimeter do
            if 2 * (length + width) == perimeter then
                count = count + 1
            end
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Count Stationery Plans That Spend a Fixed Budget on Pencils and Erasers

description.background: At a stationery store, each pencil costs pencilPrice and each eraser costs eraserPrice. A student wants to spend exactly budget.
description.task: Count the purchase plans for pencils and erasers that spend the budget exactly.
description.input: Three numbers: budget, pencilPrice, and eraserPrice.
description.output: Return the number of plans that spend the budget exactly.
description.hint: Each quantity combination needs its total price calculated before it is checked.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(budget, pencilPrice, eraserPrice)
    local count = 0

    for pencil = 0, budget do
        for eraser = 0, budget do
            local total = pencil * pencilPrice + eraser * eraserPrice
            if total == budget then
                count = count + 1
            end
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Count Game Shop Plans That Spend All Coins on Potions and Shields

description.background: In a game shop, each potion costs potionPrice coins and each shield costs shieldPrice coins. The player has coins coins and wants to spend them exactly.
description.task: Count the number of plans for buying potions and shields that spend all coins exactly.
description.input: Three numbers: coins, potionPrice, and shieldPrice.
description.output: Return the number of plans that spend all coins exactly.
description.hint: Different item quantity combinations may produce the same or different total costs.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(coins, potionPrice, shieldPrice)
    local count = 0

    for potion = 0, coins do
        for shield = 0, coins do
            local total = potion * potionPrice + shield * shieldPrice
            if total == coins then
                count = count + 1
            end
        end
    end

    return count
end

questions.title: Find the Length and Width with Maximum Area for a Fixed Perimeter

description.background: A rectangle has a fixed perimeter. The teacher wants to know which integer length-width pair has the largest area.
description.task: Find the length and width with the largest area, then return that maximum area.
description.output: Return the best length, best width, and maximum area in order.
description.hint: Enumerate all valid pairs and keep updating the current best answer.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(perimeter)
    local bestLength = 0
    local bestWidth = 0
    local bestArea = 0

    for length = 1, perimeter do
        for width = 1, perimeter do
            if 2 * (length + width) == perimeter then
                local area = length * width
                if area > bestArea then
                    bestArea = area
                    bestLength = length
                    bestWidth = width
                end
            end
        end
    end

    return bestLength, bestWidth, bestArea
end

questions.title: Chicken and Rabbit Cage: Find Animal Counts from Heads and Legs

description.background: A cage contains chickens and rabbits. Chickens have 2 legs, and rabbits have 4 legs. Given the total heads and total legs, we need to find how many chickens and rabbits there are.
description.task: Enumerate possible chicken and rabbit counts and find the answer that satisfies the conditions.
description.output: If an answer is found, return the chicken count and rabbit count in order; if no answer exists, return -1 and -1.
description.hint: Both counts are changing, and they must satisfy the head count and leg count at the same time.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(heads, legs)
    for chickens = 0, heads do
        for rabbits = 0, heads do
            if chickens + rabbits == heads and chickens * 2 + rabbits * 4 == legs then
                return chickens, rabbits
            end
        end
    end

    return -1, -1
end

questions.title: Spend the Exact Budget on Children's and Adult Tickets

description.background: At an amusement park, each child ticket costs childPrice and each adult ticket costs adultPrice. There is budget money, and the goal is to spend it exactly.
description.task: Count the purchase plans for child and adult tickets, and find the plan with the largest total ticket count.
description.input: Three numbers: budget, childPrice, and adultPrice.
description.output: Return the plan count, the child ticket count in the largest-ticket plan, and the adult ticket count in that plan.
description.hint: First decide whether a plan is valid, then decide whether it is better than the currently recorded plan.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(budget, childPrice, adultPrice)
    local count = 0
    local bestChild = 0
    local bestAdult = 0
    local bestTickets = -1

    for child = 0, budget do
        for adult = 0, budget do
            local total = child * childPrice + adult * adultPrice
            if total == budget then
                count = count + 1
                local tickets = child + adult
                if tickets > bestTickets then
                    bestTickets = tickets
                    bestChild = child
                    bestAdult = adult
                end
            end
        end
    end

    return count, bestChild, bestAdult
end

questions.title: Buy Two Products Within Budget and Maximize Quantity with Minimum Leftover

description.background: A store sells two products, with prices priceA and priceB. A student has budget money. The budget does not have to be spent exactly, but the student wants to buy as many items as possible; if the item count is tied, the student wants the least leftover money.
description.input: Three numbers: priceA, priceB, and budget.
description.output: Return the product A count, product B count, and leftover money in order.
description.hint: Split the problem into enumerating plans, checking validity, and updating the best answer.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(priceA, priceB, budget)
    local bestA = 0
    local bestB = 0
    local bestCount = -1
    local bestLeft = budget

    for a = 0, budget do
        for b = 0, budget do
            local cost = a * priceA + b * priceB
            if cost <= budget then
                local count = a + b
                local left = budget - cost

                if count > bestCount then
                    bestCount = count
                    bestLeft = left
                    bestA = a
                    bestB = b
                elseif count == bestCount and left < bestLeft then
                    bestLeft = left
                    bestA = a
                    bestB = b
                end
            end
        end
    end

    return bestA, bestB, bestLeft
end
